map = {<class 'TimeoutError'>: <class 'httpcore.ConnectTimeout'>, <class 'OSError'>: <class 'httpcore.ConnectError'>, <class 'anyio.BrokenResourceError'>: <class 'httpcore.ConnectError'>}
@contextlib.contextmanager def map_exceptions(map: ExceptionMapping) -> typing.Iterator[None]: try: yield except Exception as exc: # noqa: PIE786 for from_exc, to_exc in map.items(): if isinstance(exc, from_exc): > raise to_exc(exc) from exc E httpcore.ConnectTimeout

_ _ _ _ _ _ _
@contextlib.contextmanager def map_httpcore_exceptions() -> typing.Iterator[None]: global HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP if len(HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP) == 0: HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP = _load_httpcore_exceptions() try: yield except Exception as exc: mapped_exc = None
for from_exc, to_exc in HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP.items(): if not isinstance(exc, from_exc): continue # We want to map to the most specific exception we can find. # Eg if `exc` is an `httpcore.ReadTimeout`, we want to map to # `httpx.ReadTimeout`, not just `httpx.TimeoutException`. if mapped_exc is None or issubclass(to_exc, mapped_exc): mapped_exc = to_exc
if mapped_exc is None: # pragma: no cover raise
message = str(exc) > raise mapped_exc(message) from exc E httpx.ConnectTimeout
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/httpx/_transports/default.py:118: ConnectTimeout

testcase.name: test_chemrxiv

failure.message: AssertionError: chemrxiv should still be running after 15 seconds assert False + where False = run_function_with_timeout(<function chemrxiv at 0x10a41f2e0>, 15) + where run_function_with_timeout = <paperscraper.tests.test_dump.TestDumper object at 0x10a466b50>.run_function_with_timeout

testcase.name: test_chemrxiv_date

failure.message: requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 403 Client Error: Forbidden for url: https://chemrxiv.org/engage/chemrxiv/public-api/v1/items?limit=50&skip=0&searchDateFrom=2024-06-01&searchDateTo=2024-06-02

testcase.name: test_quantum_information_search

failure.message: AssertionError: Number of results does not match assert 7 == 4 + where 7 = len([{'factor': 25.7, 'journal': 'Innovation', 'score': 70}, {'factor': 22.5, 'journal': 'Exploration', 'score': 74}, {'fa... {'factor': 8.6, 'journal': 'International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation', 'score': 76}, ...]) + and 4 = len([{'factor': 33.2, 'journal': 'Innovation', 'score': 70}, {'factor': 22.7, 'journal': 'InfoMat', 'score': 71}, {'factor': 14.7, 'journal': 'Information Fusion', 'score': 71}, {'factor': 9.3, 'journal': 'PRX Quantum', 'score': 78}])

testcase.failure:
self = <paperscraper.tests.test_impactor.TestImpactor object at 0x10b3bb710> impactor = <paperscraper.impact.Impactor object at 0x1385c5f10>
def test_quantum_information_search(self, impactor): expected_results = [ {"journal": "Innovation", "factor": 33.2, "score": 70}, {"journal": "InfoMat", "factor": 22.7, "score": 71}, {"journal": "Information Fusion", "factor": 14.7, "score": 71}, {"journal": "PRX Quantum", "factor": 9.3, "score": 78}, ]
results = impactor.search( "Quantum information", threshold=70, sort_by="factor", min_impact=8 )
# Ensure that the results match the expected results > assert len(results) == len(expected_results), "Number of results does not match" E AssertionError: Number of results does not match E assert 7 == 4 E + where 7 = len([{'factor': 25.7, 'journal': 'Innovation', 'score': 70}, {'factor': 22.5, 'journal': 'Exploration', 'score': 74}, {'fa... {'factor': 8.6, 'journal': 'International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation', 'score': 76}, ...]) E + and 4 = len([{'factor': 33.2, 'journal': 'Innovation', 'score': 70}, {'factor': 22.7, 'journal': 'InfoMat', 'score': 71}, {'factor': 14.7, 'journal': 'Information Fusion', 'score': 71}, {'factor': 9.3, 'journal': 'PRX Quantum', 'score': 78}])
paperscraper/tests/test_impactor.py:61: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_basic_search

testcase.failure:
self = <paperscraper.tests.test_pdf.TestPDF object at 0x10b3e7650>
def test_basic_search(self): paper_data = {"doi": "10.48550/arXiv.2207.03928"} save_pdf(paper_data, filepath="gt4sd.pdf", save_metadata=True) assert os.path.exists("gt4sd.pdf") assert os.path.exists("gt4sd.json") os.remove("gt4sd.pdf") os.remove("gt4sd.json")
# # chemrxiv paper_data = {"doi": "10.26434/chemrxiv-2021-np7xj-v4"} save_pdf(paper_data, filepath="kinases.pdf", save_metadata=True) > assert os.path.exists("kinases.pdf") E AssertionError: assert False E + where False = <function exists at 0x10221b420>('kinases.pdf') E + where <function exists at 0x10221b420> = <module 'posixpath' (frozen)>.exists E + where <module 'posixpath' (frozen)> = os.path
